user_task: Standalone Video extraction (MP4 / MOV / MKV / AVI): extract speech + on-screen text from video files into a unified searchable document.
source: JTBD & user tasks tab, EXTRACT bucket — 'Standalone Video extraction'; seed queries from 'NeMo Retriever — JTBD seed queries' tab, EXTRACT → video transcription + on-screen-text → unified searchable doc.

success_criteria.quality: operational pass — (1) a video ingests via `retriever ingest <video>` (extension auto-detected, no --input-type), (2) the audio track is demuxed and transcribed (ASR speech rows), (3) sampled frames are run through page-elements + OCR for on-screen text (frame rows tagged content_type=VIDEO_FRAME), (4) speech rows + frame-OCR rows land in ONE unified row set indexed against the SAME source_id (the video path) with timestamp lineage, (5) a query surfaces content from both legs. All binary pass/fail; do NOT assert exact transcript/on-screen wording unless verified live.
success_criteria.time: slow — full extraction (speech + on-screen) <= 10 min on a 5-min clip (the bundled fixture is a ~10 s excerpt, so a live run is far under budget).
success_criteria.trigger_rate: >= 95% — a 'transcribe / make this video searchable / get spoken + on-screen text' prompt must fire the skill
success_criteria.subcommand_accuracy: >= 90% — must use `retriever ingest <video>` (auto-detected) with the correct video flags (--video-extract-audio / --video-extract-frames / --video-frame-fps / dedup) then `retriever query`; must NOT pass --input-type

environment.install: pip/uv install '.[multimedia]' (pulls the ASR/video deps) AND the system `ffmpeg` binary must be on PATH (used to demux the audio track and sample frames). Without [multimedia]+ffmpeg the video branch cannot demux/sample.
environment.prereq_note: Audio/video extraction needs the [multimedia] extra + ffmpeg per CONVENTIONS.md. Verify ffmpeg with `ffmpeg -version` before ingest.
environment.auto_detect: `retriever ingest <path>` routes by file extension. .mp4/.mov/.mkv/.avi all route to the `video` family (dry-run branch_summary `video:N`). There is NO --input-type flag.
environment.validation_path: audio track demuxed -> nvidia/parakeet-ctc-1.1b ASR (speech rows); sampled frames -> nemotron-page-elements-v3 + nemotron-ocr-v2 for on-screen text (rows tagged content_type=VIDEO_FRAME); UNIFIED row set per video: speech rows + frame-OCR rows both indexed against the SAME source_id (the video path), each row carrying source_id + timestamp lineage; --video-av-fuse fuses the audio/visual streams.

dry_run_grounding.default_video_plan: `ingest <video.mp4> --dry-run` -> branch_summary `video:1`; audio.enabled true (split_type size, split_interval 500000); audio_visual_fuse.enabled true; video_frames.enabled true, fps 0.5, dedup true (dedup_max_hamming_distance 5, dedup_max_dropped_frames 2); video_frame_text_dedup.enabled true, max_dropped_frames 2.
dry_run_grounding.frames_off_plan: `ingest <video.mp4> --video-extract-audio --no-video-extract-frames --dry-run` -> video_frames.enabled false; audio.enabled true; audio_visual_fuse.enabled true.

environment.fixture: aurora_townhall_excerpt.mp4 (~10 s, 141 KB) — the only video fixture in the catalog (/raid/retriever-sdg-v3/test-data/multiformat/mp4/). Content (speaker topic, on-screen text) is NOT asserted verbatim; tests assert structural behavior. Other container formats (.mov/.mkv/.avi) route through the identical `video` branch but are not present in the catalog.
environment.content_type_note: Frame-OCR rows are tagged content_type=VIDEO_FRAME. Query --content-types accepts text,table,chart,image,infographic at the CLI layer; assert VIDEO_FRAME tagging at the row/lineage layer, and use a plain query (no --content-types filter) so both speech and frame rows are eligible.

cases.title: Single MP4 -> speech transcript rows (audio path only, frames off)
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the EXTRACT-video task in its simplest form: one MP4 ingests via auto-detection and the audio track is demuxed + transcribed into speech rows. ADDS nothing over a baseline — frame OCR is explicitly OFF (--no-video-extract-frames), so this isolates the ASR (speech) leg of the pipeline.
cases.prompt: Transcribe data/aurora_townhall_excerpt.mp4 and tell me what the speaker's topic was. I only care about the spoken words for now.

expected_output.auto_detect: extension .mp4 auto-routes to the video family (dry-run branch_summary `video:1`); no --input-type passed
expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.rows: speech rows from nvidia/parakeet-ctc-1.1b ASR; video_frames.enabled false (no VIDEO_FRAME rows this rung)
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of hits; each {source, page_number, text}; >= 1 hit citing source=...aurora_townhall_excerpt.mp4
expected_output.answer: the spoken topic, grounded in the transcript (content NOT asserted verbatim — assert that >= 1 speech-derived hit cites the mp4 source_id)

pass_when: `retriever ingest` auto-detects the .mp4 as video (no --input-type) and completes
audio is demuxed + transcribed (speech rows present); frames OFF so no VIDEO_FRAME rows
query returns >= 1 hit whose source is the aurora_townhall_excerpt.mp4 path
ffmpeg + [multimedia] prereq satisfied (else ingest fails to demux)

cases.title: Add frame OCR -> on-screen text rows tagged VIDEO_FRAME
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the task plus the on-screen-text half. ADDS the visual leg: --video-extract-frames turns on frame sampling -> nemotron-page-elements-v3 + nemotron-ocr-v2, producing on-screen-text rows tagged content_type=VIDEO_FRAME. One new dimension over rung 1: frames now ON, so OCR'd on-screen text appears alongside the speech rows.
cases.prompt: Pull the on-screen text out of data/aurora_townhall_excerpt.mp4 (titles, slides, captions burned into the frames), not just the audio. Make it searchable.

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.rows: speech rows (parakeet ASR) PLUS frame-OCR rows; sampled frames -> page-elements-v3 -> ocr-v2; frame rows tagged content_type=VIDEO_FRAME (default video_frames.enabled true, fps 0.5, perceptual + text dedup on)
expected_output.answer: at least one returned hit is a VIDEO_FRAME (on-screen-text) row citing the mp4 source_id (on-screen wording NOT asserted verbatim)

pass_when: ingest runs with --video-extract-frames (frame sampling + OCR enabled)
>= 1 row is tagged content_type=VIDEO_FRAME (on-screen text leg produced output)
query surfaces >= 1 VIDEO_FRAME hit citing the aurora_townhall_excerpt.mp4 source_id
speech rows still present (audio leg not dropped by enabling frames)

cases.title: Unified doc: speech + frame rows under one source_id; query finds content from each
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the task's core promise: ONE unified searchable document per video. ADDS the unification assertion over rung 2 — speech rows AND VIDEO_FRAME rows must share the SAME source_id (the video path) and carry timestamp lineage, and a query must be able to surface content from BOTH legs of the same video. Exercises the 'searchable as one thing' / 'make every video in this folder searchable' seed query (single-video folder).
cases.prompt: Make every video in data/ searchable, and I need it treated as one thing: I want to find both spoken dialogue and any text shown on screen from the same video in a single search.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "<phrase that may appear in speech or on screen>" --top-k 10

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (folder ingest; branch_summary video:1)
expected_output.unification: every row from the video — speech rows (parakeet) AND VIDEO_FRAME rows (page-elements+ocr) — is indexed against the SAME source_id == the aurora_townhall_excerpt.mp4 path; each row carries timestamp lineage; --video-av-fuse fuses the audio/visual streams (audio_visual_fuse.enabled true)
expected_output.answer: the query returns hits of BOTH kinds (>=1 speech row and >=1 VIDEO_FRAME row) all citing the one mp4 source_id (specific wording NOT asserted verbatim)

pass_when: a single `retriever ingest data/` ingests the video folder (no per-file loop)
speech rows and VIDEO_FRAME rows for the video share ONE source_id (the mp4 path) — unified row set
rows carry timestamp lineage (per-row timestamp/offset present)
query surfaces content from BOTH legs (>= 1 speech hit and >= 1 VIDEO_FRAME hit) under that single source_id
cases.known_caveat: Catalog has a single video fixture, so data/ is a one-video folder; the folder-ingest path (auto-detect each video, branch_summary video:N) is exercised but with N=1. Additional .mp4/.mov/.mkv/.avi files would route the same way.

cases.title: Frame sampling / dedup controls (--video-frame-fps, --video-frame-text-dedup)
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the task while ADDING operator control over the frame-OCR leg: the agent tunes the sampling rate (--video-frame-fps) and the OCR-text dedup (--video-frame-text-dedup / --video-frame-text-dedup-max-dropped-frames) so a near-static slide is not re-OCR'd into many duplicate VIDEO_FRAME rows. New dimension over rung 3: frame sampling/dedup knobs, asserting the resolved plan reflects them and dedup collapses repeated on-screen text.
cases.prompt: data/aurora_townhall_excerpt.mp4 has long static slides. Sample frames at 1 fps but don't index the same on-screen text over and over — collapse repeated frame text. Then make it searchable.

dry_run_plan_assertions: video_frames.enabled == true AND video_frames.fps == 1.0 (sampling rate overridden from default 0.5)
video_frame_text_dedup.enabled == true AND video_frame_text_dedup.max_dropped_frames == 3 (OCR-text dedup tuned)
expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.answer: VIDEO_FRAME rows for repeated on-screen text are collapsed by dedup (fewer duplicate frame rows than an un-deduped run); query still surfaces the on-screen text once, citing the mp4 source_id

pass_when: agent inspects the resolved plan with --dry-run and confirms fps=1.0 + frame dedup + text dedup before the real run
real ingest runs with the sampling/dedup flags; speech + VIDEO_FRAME rows present
OCR-text dedup collapses repeated on-screen text (no flood of identical VIDEO_FRAME rows)
query surfaces the on-screen text citing the mp4 source_id

cases.title: Acceptance: full extraction (speech + on-screen) <= 10 min; query surfaces both with timestamp citations
cases.complexity_axis: Composed operational-pass / acceptance gate for the row: a clean end-to-end run extracts BOTH speech and on-screen text into one unified named index, finishing within the slow (<= 10 min) bucket, and a query surfaces both a spoken phrase and an on-screen phrase, each citing the video source_id with timestamp lineage. This is the gate the other rungs build up to (auto-detect + audio leg + frame leg + unification + controls, all together).
cases.prompt: Do a clean end-to-end extraction of data/aurora_townhall_excerpt.mp4 into a fresh index called 'video_smoke': get BOTH the spoken dialogue and the on-screen text into one searchable document. Then prove it: find one phrase the speaker said and one phrase shown on screen, each with a timestamp and citing the video. Confirm it finished in under 10 minutes.

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/video_smoke.
assertions: query reads the SAME table (video_smoke) the ingest wrote — not the default nemo-retriever
the unified row set holds BOTH speech rows (parakeet ASR) and VIDEO_FRAME rows (page-elements-v3 + ocr-v2), all under the one mp4 source_id
a spoken-phrase query returns >= 1 speech hit and an on-screen-phrase query returns >= 1 VIDEO_FRAME hit, each citing source=...aurora_townhall_excerpt.mp4 with timestamp lineage
every returned hit carries non-null source + page_number/timestamp
wall clock (clean state -> both queries answered) within the slow bucket <= 10 min (the ~10 s fixture finishes far sooner)

pass_when: ingest writes lancedb/video_smoke and both queries read video_smoke (table names aligned)
BOTH legs extracted: a speech hit AND a VIDEO_FRAME hit are returned, each citing the mp4 source_id with timestamp lineage
speech rows + frame rows are unified under one source_id (one searchable document)
end-to-end <= 10 min (slow bucket)
